company_type: Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) - Multifamily Apartment Communities

supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies on third-party vendors for property management services, maintenance, construction, and development activities. Supply chain constraints could result in increased development costs or delay initial occupancy dates.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain constraints may result in increased development costs or delay initial occupancy dates for all or a portion of a development community. The Company may experience supply chain constraints which could result in increased development costs or delay initial occupancy dates.

regulatory_risks: Rent control, rent stabilization, eviction, tenants' rights and similar laws and regulations could limit ability to raise rents or charge certain fees
Changes to rent control, rent stabilization, eviction, tenants' rights laws (including retroactive changes) could adversely impact results of operations and property values
New York enacted Housing Stability and Tenant Protection Act of 2019
California enacted Tenant Protection Act of 2019
City of Salinas California passed rent stabilization ordinance in September 2024
Federal, state and local governments may enact or change laws related to allowable fees and rents, eviction and other tenants' rights
Compliance with Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 could require substantial alterations and capital expenditures
Federal, state and local environmental laws governing hazardous materials, asbestos, lead-based paint, underground storage tanks, waste management
Changes in federal and state legislation and regulation on climate change may result in increased capital expenditures
Real estate tax and other laws changes could adversely affect funds from operations and ability to make distributions
State and local governments may increase property taxes or other taxes or fees
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code - must distribute at least 90% of REIT taxable income annually
Changes in U.S. federal income tax laws could have adverse impact on business and financial results
Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988 compliance requirements
State and local fire and life safety requirements
Federal, state and local accessibility requirements
